name: Fool
effect: your mind breaks and assembles many times. Your alignment changes to opposite one (LG to CE, CN to LN, TN stays the same).

name: Magician
effect: you can cast Wish spell d3 times, over the next year. (cue 'Friend like me' music, please)

name: High Priestess
effect: all your possessions now belong to archdevil Mammon. Normal items disappear into thin air, other (like real estate, for example), are now legal properties of his agents.

name: Empress
effect: you can raise one of your attributes by 2. Result can be higher than 20.

name: Emperor
effect: by magical means, you're now an owner of little fortress, its location decided by GM. By now, keep is overrun by enemies. (If there is word 'Velvet' in the name of fortress, raise your Social Link by 1).

name: Hierophant
effect: ten jewels, each worth 1,000 gp, appears before your feet.

name: Lovers
effect: decrease your Intelligence by d4 OR lose one of those abilities - darkvision, eyesight, one of your languages.

name: Chariot
effect: if you singlehandedly defeat next group of hostile enemies, you gain enough xp to advance by 1 lvl.

name: Justice
effect: place this card on a corpse to resurrect it. If over the next year resurrected person breaks the law, it dies and you become cursed.

name: Hermit
effect: once, over the next year, you can mentally ask Hermit named Igor a question. It gives you an answer and additional information to put his answer to best use.

name: Wheel of Fortune
effect: draw d6 Major Arcanas. Positive effects are doubled.

name: Strength
effect: in your sleep you meet a being that offers you power - attributes of lycanthropy, vampirism or half-dragon(ism...?).

name: Hanged Man
effect: your soul leaves your body and becomes contained in artifact, whose location is decided by DM. Artifact is immune to divination spells. It can't be 'wished' out of its place, but Wish spell can reveal its location. Artifact is defended by powerful guardian.

name: Death
effect: you die.

name: Temperance
effect: over the next year, if you are present during drawing the next card, you can cancel its effect. Doesn't work on Death and Devil.

name: Devil
effect: powerful devil or demon becomes your enemy. It starts to hunt you down, with intent to make your life miserable and, someday, kill you.

name: Tower
effect: your character is subjected to effect similar to Power Word: Pain. Spell lasts for at least one day. It can be dispelled by Greater Restoration or other spell similar in power. When this effect ends and your character was subjected to it for at least 24 hours, you gain 4 levels of exhaustion.

name: Star
effect: divine powers decide to test you by sending angelic messenger - Deva - to you. If you survive his 5 Mace attacks, you can raise two of your attributes by 1 (it can surpass 20) OR disregard this effect completely and draw another card.

name: Moon
effect: all your normal items disappear. Magical items lose their magical properties.

name: Sun
effect: you get one, random, wondrous item.

name: Judgment
effect: you can choose one event which occurred in a span of time no longer than an hour. You can decide that it never happened - reality writes itself anew and adjusts to changes in fabric of space and time.

name: World
effect: over the next year, you can cast one Time Stop. It has special rules. It lasts one round - additional rounds cost 50 HP each. During Time Stop you are subjected to Fly spell and you can also attack and use magic without breaking the spell.

name: King of Swords
effect: over the next month you are investigated by private detective, city guard or local sheriff. All instances of breaking law will be noted.

name: Queen of Swords
effect: roll d4. You become target of fanatical priest of Bane/Silvanus/Tyr/Waukeen. If you counter his charges, you will receive cleric's blessing.

name: Knight of Swords
effect: over the next week, you become really capable but also overconfident warrior. All attacks are rolled with advantage, but final result decreases by d6.

name: Knave of Swords
effect: card warns you - someone wants to fool you. For a week, your passive perception is set to 20. All deception rolls made against you have -3 penalty.

name: Ten of Swords
effect: during the next week, bad luck will join you on your path.

name: Nine of Swords
effect: you will receive news - bad news - from home or someone from your past life.

name: Eight of Swords
effect: you will be falsely accused and - probably - thrown in jail for a day or two.

name: Seven of Swords
effect: blessing of your god falls upon you. Raise one of your attributes by 2 for a week.

name: Six of Swords
effect: during next long travel you will be safe from any harm.

name: Five of Swords
effect: in the next fight, a hard-looking enemy will be slain surprisingly fast - only to get back up with +50% HP.

name: Four of Swords
effect: treat next short rest as a long one.

name: Three of Swords
effect: during the next stop at inn or tavern, you will be treated like persona non grata.

name: Two of Swords
effect: in the next fight, sum of HP of enemies will be equal to that of the party.

name: Ace of Swords
effect: rejoice! You will encounter a jousting tournament held in the name of a local lord. All challengers are encouraged to join - prize will be grand!

name: King of Wands
effect: you will meet a friendly cleric/mage, but his patience for you will be short.

name: Queen of Wands
effect: you will meet a woman that will treat you like a son.

name: Knight of Wands
effect: through the next day, you can Levitate at will.

name: Knave of Wands
effect: during the next fight, you will get help from an unexpected ally.

name: Ten of Wands
effect: a thief will set his eyes on the party's possessions.

name: Nine of Wands
effect: you will catch a disease. Roll d6. Symptoms are: deafness/blindness/diarrhea/narcolepsy/hallucinations/coughing blood.

name: Eight of Wands
effect: for a week, your speed doubles.

name: Seven of Wands
effect: for the next week, luck has sided with you.

name: Six of Wands
effect: over the next week, you can cast Little Wish. It works analogically to Wish spell, but is weaker (for example, it can copy effects of a 3rd, not 8th, level spell).

name: Five of Wands
effect: the next place you will visit will suffer famine.

name: Four of Wands
effect: a random NPC will fall in love with your character.

name: Three of Wands
effect: keep this card. If you give it to a merchant during your next transaction, you will get bonus gold.

name: Two of Wands
effect: the next visited village will be cursed by a powerful deity, because villagers stopped treating its shrine or temple.

name: Ace of Wands
effect: local area will be devastated by cataclysm - flood, whirlwind, earthquake. It will bring great losses, but also unearth a lost, unexplored place - cave, underground temple, sunken island etc.

name: King of Cups
effect: you will meet a diplomat traveling from/to the king's court. He heard about your accomplishments (if there are any).

name: Queen of Cups
effect: you will find a village devastated by cataclysm/wild animals/other danger. The only person that tries to help peasants is a sole cleric of Ilmater.

name: Knight of Cups
effect: envy, ahoy! You will meet a rival adventuring party, jealous of your successes.

name: Knave of Cups
effect: a trade factory, deep in the wilderness, needs an associate. Money's in the air.

name: Ten of Cups
effect: roll d10 3 times. If the result matches your alignment (1 - LG, 2 - NG, 3 - CG... 10 - reroll), you will meet a cleric of a deity with your alignment that will give you his blessing.

name: Nine of Cups
effect: you can give this card to anyone. It raises Magician's card result by 1.

name: Eight of Cups
effect: next investment - trade, journey to dungeon, searching for treasure - will bring no profit.

name: Seven of Cups
effect: for the next week, you will have illusions of treasures, enemies and dangers.

name: Six of Cups
effect: you can change one thing in your character sheet - take a different feat, decide about another way of gaining HP, etc. You can't change your race, class, background. You can change alignment only if it is different than the starting one.

name: Five of Cups
effect: you are overwhelmed by pessimism. Until the end of the session, you have disadvantage on all saving throws and ability checks.

name: Four of Cups
effect: you will meet a meditating monk. If treated in the right way, the monk will be willing to do one favour for you.

name: Three of Cups
effect: huge fair in the next village or city. All inns will be full. The town will also be full... of thieves and charlatans.

name: Two of Cups
effect: you are filled with love for all living things. For the next week you can't kill anybody - only knock them out.

name: Ace of Cups
effect: you will be targeted by a succubus, who will try to take your love, your vital force and your life.

name: King of Pentacles
effect: the next treasure you find will be 2 times bigger.

name: Queen of Pentacles
effect: during your travels, you will get a letter from a widowed baroness. She will ask you for help in fulfilling the will of her late husband.

name: Knight of Pentacles
effect: you will find a village of pitch burners. For help with their next big order, they will polish your armor with magical dust. Armor gets +1 AC.

name: Knave of Pentacles
effect: you feel that you can learn something new. If you put some work into it, you can raise one of your proficiencies by 1.

name: Ten of Pentacles
effect: if during your travels you helped any business, it's time to get your dividends.

name: Nine of Pentacles
effect: in the next inn, only the most expensive room is free (40 gp for a night). It's a really comfortable apartment though - after a night inside, you get inspiration.

name: Eight of Pentacles
effect: in the next town you meet a guildmaster/master craftsman. For 50 gp and spending two days in town, he can teach you the use of one kind of tools.

name: Seven of Pentacles
effect: in the next inn, you meet a dwarf that offers you purchase of stock in a magical forge near Phandalin. Price is equal to 100 gp per stock. Every day you can roll d20 for stock change. At 10, price doesn't change. At 11 rises by 5%, at 9 - drops by 5% etc.

name: Six of Pentacles
effect: on your way, you meet a Lathander's cleric on his pilgrimage. You can support his cause with money. Regardless, it has no further effect.

name: Five of Pentacles
effect: if you have any jewels on you, their value drops by 50%.

name: Four of Pentacles
effect: you meet a traveling merchant that offers you magical weapons at a discount. The next day you find out that Distort Value was cast on them - they're just normal, non-magical items.

name: Three of Pentacles
effect: the party will find a barrel marked as 'lamp oil'. Inside is trapped an aggressive djinni. If the party defeats him, the next time anyone picks the Magician card, its result is raised by 1.

name: Two of Pentacles
effect: if the party traverses the next dungeon without any short or long rest, the treasure will magically double itself.

name: Ace of Pentacles
effect: a big chunk of rock falls from the skies. Quick! Roll Dexterity ST, DC 18. Failure - 10d10 damage. Success - damage is halved. If the party decides to check it, it turns out there is a vein of mithril inside worth 5,000 gp.
